title: trifolium pratense var. baeticum
intro: Trifolium pratense var. baeticum, also known as red clover, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and estrogenic effects due to the presence of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of red clover are attributed to its ability to support cardiovascular health, alleviate menopausal symptoms, and may also have a potential role in reducing the risk of osteoporosis and certain types of cancer. Red clover is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture for its medicinal use, and it can also be consumed as a dietary supplement or added to food products as a natural ingredient.
